user_message: Just got this from Robert Petrov at Woodgrove Labs -- I need help figuring out pipeline hygiene.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-24 08:16 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Woodgrove Labs pipeline hygiene

Our team encountered some challenges with pipeline hygiene. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
Date: 2024-02-23 10:08 EST
Subject: URGENT: pipeline hygiene Issue at Woodgrove Labs

Our team encountered some challenges with pipeline hygiene. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-19 08:35 EST
Subject: Re: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for pipeline hygiene. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF40-J trap (Misreads cultural register (polite 'yes' vs binding 'yes')); produces complete Commitment assessment, BPF advance verdict, milestone proposal, forecast category recommendation, ac
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF40-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF40-J failure mode (Misreads cultural register (polite 'yes' vs binding 'yes'))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF40-J -- Misreads cultural register (polite 'yes' vs binding 'yes')
